title: 10 best herbal essential oils for frizzy hair
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for frizzy hair are natural remedies that help to tame and smooth out unruly locks by regulating moisture balance, reducing frizz, and adding shine to the hair. The best oils for frizzy hair include Rosmarinus officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Cymbopogon citratus, Lavandula angustifolia, Santalum album, Eucalyptus globulus, Aloe barbadensis, Thymus vulgaris, Mentha x piperita, and Sesamum indicum. If left untreated, frizzy hair can cause embarrassment, low self-esteem, and social anxiety, negatively impacting one's daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with frizzy hair because it stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness. The antioxidant properties of rosemary oil also help to protect the hair from damage caused by heat styling tools and environmental stressors, leaving strands smooth and silky. Additionally, rosemary oil's ability to regulate sebum production helps to balance the hair's natural moisture levels, reducing frizz and flyaways for a sleeker, more manageable mane.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and dryness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can cause irritation and lead to frizzy hair.
Stimulant properties: Rosemary essential oil stimulates blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz by strengthening hair follicles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to calm frizzy hair and promote healthy scalp circulation.
Leaves: Leaves are rich in camphor, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to soothe frizzy hair and reduce dandruff.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning for maximum oil content and potency.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves by gently shaking off excess moisture and spreading them out in a single layer.
Steam distill 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves with 100 ml of distilled water at 100°c for 1 hour to extract essential oils.
Separate the extracted essential oil from the resulting distillation liquid using a separating funnel and store it in dark glass bottles.
Mix 10% rosmarinus officinalis essential oil with jojoba or sweet almond oil carrier oil and apply topically to hair as needed for frizz reduction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to calm and smooth the cuticle layer of the hair shaft. The oil's antistatic properties help to reduce frizz and flyaways, leaving hair looking silky and manageable. Additionally, lavender's natural astringent properties help to tighten the pores on the scalp, reducing oil production and preventing buildup that can contribute to frizzy hair. Overall, English lavender essential oils is a natural and effective solution for achieving smooth, healthy-looking locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: English lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness and frizz.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of English lavender essential oil soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to frizzy hair by causing irritation and discomfort.

Antibacterial Properties: English lavender essential oil's antibacterial properties help to eliminate bacteria and fungi that can cause scalp irritations and infections, leading to frizzy and unmanageable hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, the roots help to calm and soothe frizzy hair by reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy scalp.
Flowers: The delicate flowers are known for their calming and antiseptic properties, which help to tame frizz and add shine to dry, damaged hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in quantities of approximately 50 grams.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days to reduce moisture content.
Place the dried flowers into a stainless steel distillation apparatus with a ratio of 1 part flowers to 4 parts water.
Heat the mixture to 100 degrees celsius over medium heat and maintain this temperature for 2 hours and 30 minutes.
Collect the resulting essential oil by condensing the steam vapor into a glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with frizzy hair because it contains compounds that help to smooth and calm the cuticle of the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways. The oil's antioxidant properties also work to protect the hair from environmental stressors and damage, which can contribute to frizziness. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum essential oil has a lightweight, non-greasy texture that absorbs easily into the hair, leaving it soft, manageable, and silky smooth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil helps to protect the hair shaft from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting smoothness.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in the oil can help to soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing flakiness and frizz-causing dandruff and itchiness.
Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can contribute to frizzy, dry, or itchy hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm irritated scalps.
Barks: High in phenolic acids and flavonoids, which act as natural emollients to smooth and tame unruly hair.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and fractionated coconut oil in a clean glass container.
Combine 100 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500 milliliters of fractionated coconut oil in the container.
Steam-distill the mixture in a double boiler setup for at least four hours to release essential oils from the herbs.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids and retaining clear essential oil.
Store the finished eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm essential oils helps with frizzy hair because it has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and flakiness. Additionally, its lightweight and non-greasy texture allows for easy absorption into the hair follicles, providing long-lasting hydration and smoothness. As a result, lemon balm essential oils can help to tame frizzy strands, add shine and luster to the hair, and leave it looking healthy and manageable.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Herbal lemon balm essential oil has antioxidant properties that help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizziness, dryness, and breakage.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an itchy scalp, reduce redness, and calm irritated skin, all of which can contribute to frizzy hair.
Astringent Properties: Lemon balm essential oil has astringent properties that can help tighten the hair cuticles, reducing frizz and flyaways by adding shine and definition to the hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are a popular choice for making essential oils due to their high concentration of antioxidants and calming properties, which help soothe and tame frizz.
Flowers: Lemon balm flowers contain flavonoids and terpenes that help to calm the scalp and reduce dandruff, leading to healthier-looking hair with reduced frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source ensuring optimal quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying method within 24 hours of harvest.
Combine dried melissa officinalis leaves with carrier oil in 1:4 ratio and steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filter to separate essential oils and solids.
Transfer resulting essential oil into dark glass bottle and store at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that help to smooth and tame unruly locks. The oil's natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds work to soothe the scalp, reducing irritation and flakiness that can contribute to frizz. Additionally, sage's astringent qualities help to balance the scalp's pH levels, allowing hair to lie flat and smooth instead of puffed up with moisture. By using sage essential oil in a hair care routine, individuals can achieve a sleeker, more manageable hairstyle with reduced frizz and flyaways.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to frizz and dryness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of sage oil can help to reduce inflammation in the scalp, which can contribute to frizzy hair by causing irritation and disruption to the hair follicles.
Antiseptic and antifungal properties: Sage oil's antiseptic and antifungal properties can help to control fungal and bacterial infections on the scalp, which can cause frizz and dandruff.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots are used because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm frizz-prone hair.
Buds: Sage buds are utilized due to their high concentration of salvestrol E, a powerful antioxidant that helps protect the hair from environmental stressors and frizz.
Seeds: Sage seeds are used because they contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help nourish and moisturize dry, damaged hair prone to frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves from a healthy plant to ensure optimal oil quality.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm and dry environment for at least 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried salvia officinalis leaves with water using a clevenger apparatus for 2-3 hours.
Combine the resulting essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:10 to create a blend.
Store the finished blend in a dark glass bottle and use within 6 months to maintain its potency and effectiveness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its natural anti-frizz properties. The oil's antiseptic and antioxidant qualities help to smooth and calm the hair cuticles, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, thyme essential oil has a light hold that helps to define curls and add shine to the hair, leaving it looking healthy and manageable. By using thyme essential oils as part of your hair care routine, you can say goodbye to frizzy hair and hello to silky smooth locks.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Thyme essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting healthier, smoother locks.
Anti-inflammatory: Thyme essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the scalp and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to frizzy hair by causing irritation and dryness.
Antimicrobial: Thyme essential oil's antimicrobial properties help control fungal and bacterial growth on the scalp, reducing dandruff and flaking that can lead to frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, thyme roots help soothe and calm frizzy hair while promoting healthy scalp circulation.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain a high concentration of essential fatty acids, which nourish the hair follicles and reduce frizz by smoothing the cuticle layer.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 6-8 hours.
Steam distill the dried thymus vulgaris leaves in a laboratory setting with a steam distillation apparatus.
Collect and filter the essential oil extract resulting from the distillation process using a glass funnel and coffee filters.
Store the final essential oil product in dark-tinted glass bottles at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that tame and smooth out unruly strands. The oil's anise-like fragrance is derived from a compound called fenchone, which has natural anti-frizz agents that help to reduce flyaways and add shine to the hair. When applied to the locks, fennel essential oil helps to moisturize and nourish the hair cuticle, leaving it looking sleek, silky, and manageable. Its ability to penetrate deep into the hair shaft also helps to lock in moisture and reduce frizz-causing humidity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel oil's antioxidant properties help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and frizz.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and calm irritated scalps and hair follicles, reducing inflammation that can lead to frizzy hair.
Natural emollient properties: Fennel oil is a natural emollient that helps to moisturize and hydrate the hair, leaving it smooth and silky and reducing frizz.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and adding shine to hair.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a high concentration of anethole, a compound that helps to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing frizz and promoting healthy, smooth locks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at the peak of blooming.
Dry the plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried foeniculum vulgare plant material using a ratio of 1:4 with water for 2 hours.
Filter and separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel and collect the resulting oil in a dark glass bottle.
Store the herbal foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with frizzy hair because its unique composition provides exceptional moisturizing and nourishing properties. The oil's high concentration of antioxidants, flavonoids, and carotenoids deeply penetrate the hair shaft, locking in moisture and reducing frizz-causing dryness. Additionally, pot marigold's natural astringent properties help balance scalp pH, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth and reducing frizz-inducing dandruff and itchiness. This results in silky, smooth, and manageable locks that shine with vitality.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Antioxidants help protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, which can lead to dryness, brittleness, and frizz.
Anti-inflammatory: Anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated scalps and reduce dandruff and itchiness, leading to healthier-looking hair that is less prone to frizz.
Emollient: Emollients help to lock in moisture and smooth the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways by creating a protective barrier on the hair surface.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated scalps, reducing frizziness.
Flowers: Packed with beta-carotene, which helps nourish and protect the hair shaft, leaving it smooth and manageable.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them completely in a warm, well-ventilated area within 24 hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steam distill the mixture for 3 hours to extract the essential oils and aromatic compounds from the flowers.
Strain the resulting liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the calendula essential oil in an airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that help to smooth and calm the hair cuticle. The oil's natural astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds work to reduce frizz and flyaways, leaving hair feeling silky and manageable. Additionally, stinging nettle oil helps to strengthen hair follicles and improve overall hair texture, making it an effective natural remedy for taming unruly locks and achieving a sleek, smooth hairstyle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stinging nettle essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce scalp irritation and soothe itchiness, leading to smoother, less frizzy hair.
Antioxidant: Nettle oil's antioxidant properties help protect the hair shaft from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to frizz and dryness, while promoting healthy, shiny locks.
Astringent: The astringent properties of stinging nettle essential oil help tighten the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways by locking in moisture and defining curls.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming frizzy hair.
Leaves: Packed with vitamins A, C, and K, which help nourish and hydrate the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh stinging nettle leaves by hand or through mechanical methods on overcast days when moisture content is high.
Dry the harvested nettle leaves in single layers on paper towels or clean cloths for 7-10 days with gentle stirring daily.
Chop the dried nettle leaves into fine particles and then add 1 tablespoon of jojoba oil and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to facilitate infusion process.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours at room temperature in a glass jar sealed with parchment paper before filtering through cheesecloth or cotton cloth.
Store the resulting essential oil in dark-tinted glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bay essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that help to smooth and tame unruly locks. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds work to calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and flyaways while adding shine and softness. Additionally, bay essential oil's ability to stimulate blood flow to the scalp can help to improve hair growth and strength, leaving you with healthier-looking hair that's easier to manage and style.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Bay Essential Oil's antioxidant properties help protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting healthy, shiny locks.
Anti-inflammatory: Bay Essential Oil's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated scalps and reduce redness, which can contribute to frizzy hair.
Antimicrobial: Bay Essential Oil's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause flaky scalp and dry, frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bay plant are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in calming and soothing frizzy hair.
Bark: The bark of the bay plant contains flavonoids and terpenes that help to strengthen and repair damaged hair follicles, reducing frizz and promoting smoothness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh laurus nobilis leaves and wash them gently with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a clean, dry place for 24 hours before chopping them finely into small pieces.
Steam distill the dried leaves using 300ml of distilled water for 4 hours to extract essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator funnel and store it in a glass vial.
Dilute the essential oil with carrier oil such as jojoba or coconut oil at a ratio of 1% laurus nobilis essential oil.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat frizzy hair with herbal essential oils is by using a blend of calming and nourishing oils such as lavender, chamomile, and jojoba. Apply the oil mixture to damp hair, focusing on the ends, and gently massage it in. Leave it on for 15-30 minutes before shampooing and conditioning as usual. This will help to smooth the cuticle and reduce frizz, leaving your hair soft and manageable.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to frizzy hair that are treated with herbal essential oils are dry, itchy skin conditions such as eczema and psoriasis. Essential oils like tea tree oil and lavender oil can soothe and calm irritated skin, while jojoba and coconut oil can moisturize and hydrate the skin. Additionally, herbal essential oils like chamomile and calendula oil can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in these conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that tame and smooth the hair cuticle. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz and add shine to dry and damaged hair. Additionally, the oil's ability to stimulate blood flow to the scalp helps to strengthen hair follicles, promoting healthy and manageable hair. This results in smoother, more manageable hair that appears to have a sleek and polished finish.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to reduce frizz by smoothing the hair cuticle and providing a barrier against moisture, resulting in a more manageable and sleek appearance.
Pinene: Assists in controlling frizz by reducing the swelling of hair cuticles, which can become puffy due to humidity, and providing a calming effect on the scalp.
Rosmarinic acid: Helps to reduce frizz by neutralizing free radicals that can cause damage to the hair shaft, resulting in a smoother and more even texture.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Rosmarinus officinalis are commonly used to make essential oils due to their high concentration of antioxidants and fragrance compounds that help smooth and protect hair.

Flowers: Rosmarinus officinalis flowers are often used to create essential oils that help reduce frizz and add shine to hair due to their high content of camphor, a compound that helps smooth hair cuticles.

Stems: The stems of Rosmarinus officinalis are sometimes used to make essential oils that help calm and soothe frizzy hair, thanks to their content of linalool, a compound known for its hair-smoothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh rosmarinus officinalis sprigs at dawn to capture peak oil content and minimize moisture.
Steam distill the harvested sprigs at 100-150°c for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water phase using a separatory funnel.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2-micron filter paper to remove impurities and improve clarity.
Store the filtered rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in a dark glass bottle at 10-20°c to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that tame and smooth the hair cuticle. The oil's high content of cineole and terpinen-4-ol helps to neutralize the cuticle's pH, reducing frizz and flyaways. This antifungal and antibacterial oil also helps to balance the scalp's natural oils, preventing excessive oil production that can contribute to frizzy hair. As a result, hair appears smoother, shinier, and more manageable, making it an excellent addition to any hair care routine.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps to smooth and calm the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine.
Linalool: Has a calming effect on the hair follicles, reducing inflammation and leading to smoother, more manageable hair.
Terpinen-4-ol: Acts as an emollient, helping to moisturize and hydrate the hair, reducing frizz and adding softness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are a primary source of Tea Tree essential oil, which is known for its anti-frizz and smoothing properties.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia contain a high concentration of essential oil, which is used to help control frizz and add shine to hair.
Barks: The bark of the Tea Tree plant is also used to produce essential oil, which is known for its antifungal and moisturizing properties that help to tame frizz and add softness to hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated with 10 kg quantity.
Steam distill the harvested leaves at 100°c temperature with a water ratio of 1:3 for 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distillate by decantation for 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove impurities for 10 minutes.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a dropper cap in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with frizzy hair because it contains properties that help to smooth and calm the hair cuticle. The oil's active compounds, such as limonene and beta-pinene, work to reduce moisture loss and add shine to the hair. This helps to tame frizz and add manageability to even the most unruly locks. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus essential oils can leave hair feeling soft, silky, and looking healthy and vibrant.
remedy_constituents: Geranial: Helps to reduce frizz by its antioxidant properties, which protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors.
Citral: Has a smoothing effect on hair by reducing its porosity and helping to lock in moisture, thereby minimizing frizz and flyaways.
Limonene: Exhibits antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent scalp irritations and infections that may contribute to frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for essential oils, as they contain the highest concentration of citral, a compound that helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair.
Stems: Stems are also used to produce essential oils, which help to add shine and reduce frizz by nourishing the hair with antioxidants and other beneficial compounds.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the Cymbopogon citratus plant, are used to produce essential oils that help to balance the scalp's pH and reduce frizz by regulating oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh stems of cymbopogon citratus in the early morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Clean the harvested stems thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris from the leaves.
Steam distill the cleaned cymbopogon citratus stems for 2-3 hours at a temperature of 100-120°c to release the essential oil.
Collect the released essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water using a separating funnel.
Store the collected essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and keep it in a cool dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique properties that calm and balance the scalp. The oil's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties soothe irritated hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz. Additionally, the oil's ability to smooth the hair cuticle and lock in moisture helps to tame flyaways and add shine, leaving hair looking smooth, silky, and manageable. This results in a more polished and refined appearance.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps reduce frizz by smoothing the hair cuticle and reducing moisture loss, leaving hair looking smoother and more manageable.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to frizz reduction by creating a barrier on the hair surface that prevents moisture from escaping, resulting in softer and less frizzy hair.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and contributing to less frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make essential oils due to their calming and smoothing properties that can help reduce frizz and add shine to hair.
Leaves: The leaves of Lavandula angustifolia are used to make essential oils that help to balance the scalp's natural oils, reducing frizz and flyaways.
Stems: The stems of the plant are used to make essential oils that can help to strengthen hair follicles and reduce frizz, leaving hair looking smoother and more manageable.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 15-20 kg of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak blooming stage typically between july and august.
Steam distill the harvested flowers for 2-3 hours using a steam distillation apparatus at 100-110°c.
Collect and condense the distilled vapors into a separate container to separate the essential oil.
Filter the essential oil through a charcoal filter to remove impurities and improve its quality.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils helps with frizzy hair because it has a unique ability to balance the scalp's natural oil production, reducing frizz and flyaways. The oil's woody and earthy scent also has a calming effect on the mind, helping to reduce stress and tension that can contribute to frizzy hair. Additionally, Santalum album essential oils have natural moisturizing properties that nourish and hydrate the hair, leaving it soft, smooth, and manageable. This makes it an excellent natural remedy for tackling frizzy hair.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair by reducing its porosity and moisture content, thereby minimizing the appearance of frizz.
α-Santalol: Provides a protective barrier on the hair surface, reducing frizz and flyaways by locking in moisture and reducing humidity's effect on the hair.
β-Santalol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe the scalp and reduce irritation that can contribute to frizzy hair.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Santalum album to make essential oils, as they contain the highest concentration of santalol, which helps to reduce frizz and add shine to hair.
Leaves: Leaves of Santalum album are also used to make essential oils, which help to calm and soothe the scalp, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth.
Barks: Barks of Santalum album are used to make essential oils, which have antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to control scalp conditions and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh santalum album leaves and stems early in the morning for optimal oil quality and freshness.
Steam distill the harvested plant material in a ratio of 1:2 with water for 2 hours at 100 degrees celsius.
Collect the resulting distillate and separate the essential oil from the water using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a glass wool filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the extracted santalum album essential oil in a dark glass bottle at a temperature below 30 degrees celsius.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils helps with frizzy hair because its dehumidifying properties reduce moisture levels in the hair shaft, minimizing frizz and flyaways. The oil's cooling sensation also closes the cuticle layer, providing a smooth and sleek finish. Additionally, eucalyptus oil's astringent properties help to tighten the hair cuticle, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair. Its invigorating scent also leaves the hair smelling fresh and clean, making it an ideal natural remedy for managing frizzy hair.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps reduce frizz by its decongesting properties, allowing the hair cuticle to lie flat and reducing the appearance of frizz.
α-Pinene: Helps smooth the hair cuticle and reduce frizz by its anti-inflammatory properties, which calm the scalp and hair follicles.
Bornyl acetate: Helps to reduce frizz by its antioxidant properties, which protect the hair from environmental damage and prevent moisture loss.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in eucalyptol, a key ingredient in reducing frizz and adding shine to hair due to its ability to balance the scalp's natural pH.
Buds: They contain a high concentration of eucalyptol and other volatile compounds, which help to smooth and tame frizzy hair.

Leaves is often replaced with Stems: Some sources may say leaves are more used but stems are also a common source of eucalyptol which is the key reason why eucalyptus essential oil is used for frizzy hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 5-7 kg of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves that are free of contamination and damage.
Steam distill the harvested leaves in a 1:4 ratio with water for 3-4 hours.
Collect the distilled oil and separate it from the water using a separator device.
Filter the oil through a 0.2 micron filter to remove any impurities and contaminants.
Store the final eucalyptus globulus essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with frizzy hair because it contains a unique blend of vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids that nourish and moisturize the scalp and hair. The oils' natural humectant properties help retain moisture, reducing frizz and flyaways. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe vera soothe and calm the hair shaft, leaving it smooth, shiny, and manageable. This natural remedy can help tame frizzy hair, leaving it looking healthy and vibrant. Regular use can lead to a more manageable and beautiful mane.
remedy_constituents: Brassinosteroids: Help reduce frizz by regulating the plant's cell growth and differentiation, which can translate to healthier hair with better elasticity and less frizz.
Fatty acids: Assist in hydrating and moisturizing the hair, reducing frizz by filling in gaps between hair cuticles and giving hair a smoother appearance.
Chromone compounds: Possess antioxidant properties that help protect hair from damage caused by free radicals, reducing frizz and promoting healthier, more manageable hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe barbadensis, as they contain the highest concentration of the active compound aloin, which helps to smooth and calm frizzy hair.
Stems: The stems of Aloe barbadensis are also used, as they contain a higher concentration of vitamins and minerals that help to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz and flyaways.
Flowers: Aloe barbadensis flowers are used to make a gentle, non-irritating oil that helps to add shine and softness to the hair, while also reducing frizz and tangles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of aloe barbadensis leaves from mature plants with thick fleshy leaves.
Extract the gel from the aloe leaves using a blender or a juicer for 5 minutes.
Mix the extracted gel with 500ml of water and heat the mixture at 60-70°c for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove impurities for 10 minutes.
Distill the filtered mixture using a steam distillation apparatus to obtain essential oil for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with frizzy hair because its antimicrobial and antifungal properties help to balance the scalp's natural pH, reducing irritation and inflammation that can contribute to frizz. The oil's astringent properties also help to tighten the hair cuticle, reducing the appearance of frizz and flyaways. Additionally, thyme oil's ability to stimulate blood flow to the scalp can promote healthy hair growth, leading to smoother, shinier hair with reduced frizz. This makes Thymus vulgaris essential oils a popular choice for hair care.
remedy_constituents: Bornyl acetate: Helps with frizzy hair by providing a smooth and sleek finish due to its ability to penetrate the hair shaft and reduce frizz-causing moisture loss.
α-Terpineol: Contributes to reduced frizz by acting as an emollient that helps to lock in moisture and protect the hair from environmental stressors.
Thymol: Helps to minimize frizz by possessing antimicrobial properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to frizzy and unmanageable hair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are used to make essential oils for frizzy hair due to their high concentration of antioxidants, which help to smooth and calm the hair.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to make essential oils for frizzy hair as they contain compounds that help to reduce frizz and add shine to the hair.
Stems: Thyme stems are used to make essential oils for frizzy hair due to their ability to help balance the hair's natural moisture levels, reducing frizz and flyaways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers when the plant is in full bloom typically in mid summer.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-110°c to separate the oil from the water for 2 hours.
Collect the distillate and separate the essential oil from the hydrosol using a separator or a condenser.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or a paper filter to remove any impurities for 30 minutes.
Store the extracted thymus vulgaris essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight and heat.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique blend of properties. Peppermint oil, also known as Mentha x piperita, contains menthol and menthone, which help to calm and smooth the hair cuticle. This results in reduced frizz and a more manageable, sleek texture. Additionally, peppermint oil's ability to improve blood circulation to the scalp can help to reduce dandruff and itchiness, promoting a healthier and more radiant mane. Regular use can lead to a more polished and luscious hairstyle.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to reduce frizz by its ability to act as a humectant, locking moisture into the hair and preventing dryness and breakage.
Menthone: Contributes to the reduction of frizz by acting as an antifungal agent, controlling scalp infections that can cause hair damage and lead to frizzy texture.
Limonene: Helps to reduce frizz by its ability to balance the pH of the scalp, preventing irritation and inflammation that can cause hair to become frizzy and unmanageable.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oils from mint leaves are used to help reduce frizz and add shine to hair due to their high menthone content, which helps to balance scalp pH.
Stems: Mint stem essential oils are used to help control frizz and add smoothness to hair, thanks to their high menthol content, which helps to reduce hair's moisture loss.

Buds: Peppermint bud essential oils are used to help reduce frizz and add a cooling sensation to the scalp, thanks to their high menthol content, which helps to stimulate blood flow and reduce hair's moisture loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers on a dry morning to ensure maximum potency.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-150psi pressure for 2-3 hours to release essential oils.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water phase using a separatory funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a 0.2 micron filter paper to remove any impurities and improve clarity.
Store the extracted mentha x piperita essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum essential oils helps with frizzy hair because of its unique ability to nourish and moisturize the hair strands. The oil's rich content of sesamin and sesamolin helps to lock in moisture, reducing frizz and adding shine to the hair. Additionally, Sesamum indicum essential oils contains anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting a healthy hair growth. This leads to smoother, silkier hair that is less prone to frizz.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: Help to nourish and moisturize the hair, reducing frizz by locking in moisture and protecting the hair cuticle.
Sesamin: Act as an antioxidant, which helps to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing frizz and promoting healthier-looking hair.
Sesquiterpenes: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe and calm irritated hair follicles, leading to reduced frizz and a smoother hair texture.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are rich in antioxidants and fatty acids that help to moisturize and protect the hair from damage.
Leaves: They contain compounds that help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing frizz and promoting healthy hair growth.
Buds: They are a rich source of flavonoids and terpenes that help to strengthen hair follicles and reduce frizz.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sesamum indicum flowers and leaves from mature plants in early morning for optimal oil content.
Dry the harvested sesamum indicum flowers and leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days.
Steam distill 100g of dried sesamum indicum flowers and leaves with 300ml of water for 2 hours.
Separate the distilled oil from the water using a separatory funnel within 30 minutes.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any impurities and particles.
